task_completion.description: Open the BookingService.java file in Eclipse and locate the createBooking method.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eclipse IDE is open with the FlightSystem project loaded. The BookingService.java file is open in the editor, displaying the createBooking method with its original 8 parameters.

task_completion.description: Trigger the 'Introduce Parameter Object' refactoring dialog for the createBooking method.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Introduce Parameter Object' dialog box is visible on the screen, overlaid on the Eclipse IDE.

task_completion.description: Configure the new parameter object with the correct class name and selected parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the refactoring dialog, the class name is set to 'SeatDetails'. The parameters 'seatRow', 'seatLetter', 'window', 'aisle', and 'exitRow' are selected/checked to be included in the new object, while 'passengerId', 'flightNumber', and 'flightDate' are left unselected/separate.

task_completion.description: Apply the refactoring and verify the createBooking method signature is updated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BookingService.java editor shows the createBooking method signature now accepts a 'SeatDetails' object instead of the 5 individual seat parameters. The 3 non-seat parameters remain in the signature.

task_completion.description: Verify that BookingApp.java is automatically updated and the project compiles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: BookingApp.java is open in the editor, showing the instantiation of the new 'SeatDetails' object being passed into the createBooking method call. There are no red squiggly lines or error markers in the file or project explorer, indicating successful compilation.

integrity.description: The agent used Eclipse's built-in refactoring tool rather than manually typing the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the 'Introduce Parameter Object' dialog and automated changes across multiple files (BookingService.java and BookingApp.java) rather than manual text editing line-by-line.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Eclipse IDE application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE interface (Package Explorer, standard Eclipse toolbars, and editor tabs) is clearly visible. The agent did not use a basic text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands (like sed or awk) to perform the refactoring.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing script-based text replacement commands targeting the Java files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The FlightSystem project is located at /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/FlightSystem. The original createBooking method in BookingService.java has 8 parameters: passengerId, flightNumber, flightDate, seatRow, seatLetter, window, aisle, exitRow. The refactoring must group exactly the 5 seat-related parameters (seatRow, seatLetter, window, aisle, exitRow) into a new class named 'SeatDetails'. The parameters passengerId, flightNumber, and flightDate must remain as separate arguments in the method signature. The refactoring should automatically update BookingApp.java to instantiate SeatDetails.
